Abijam’s heart was not completely devoted to the Lord his God. He wanted sincerity; he started well, but he strayed from that path and walked in all the sins of his father, following his bad example, even though he had seen the negative consequences of it. David's family continued as a light in Jerusalem, to maintain true worship of God there, when the light of Divine truth was extinguished everywhere else. The Lord has still taken care of his cause, while those who should have been helpful to it have lived and died in their sins. The Son of David will continue to be a light to his church, to establish it in truth and righteousness until the end of time. There are two ways of fulfilling the law, one legal, the other by the gospel. Legal fulfillment is when people do all things required by the law, and they do them themselves. No one ever fulfilled the law this way except Christ, and Adam before his fall. The gospel way of fulfilling the law is to believe in Christ who fulfilled the law for us, and to strive with your whole being to obey God in all his commands. This is accepted by God, as it is for all those who are in Christ. Thus David and others are said to fulfill the law.

Asa did what was right in the eyes of the Lord. That is truly right which is so in God’s eyes. Asa's times were times of reformation. He removed that which was evil; that is where reformation begins, and he found a great deal needed to be done. When Asa found idolatry in the court, he eliminated it from there. Reformation must begin at home. Asa honors and respects his mother; he loves her well, but he loves God better. Those who have power are happy when they have hearts to use it wisely. We must not only stop doing evil, but learn to do good; not only cast away the idols of our iniquity, but dedicate ourselves and all that we have to God’s honor and glory. Asa was wholeheartedly devoted to the service of God, his sins not arising from presumption. But his agreement with Benhadad arose from unbelief. Even true believers find it difficult, in times of urgent danger, to trust in the Lord with all their heart. Unbelief opens the way for worldly strategies, and thus one sin after another. Unbelief has often led Christians to call on the help of the Lord's enemies in their conflicts with their brothers; and some who once shone brightly have been covered with a dark cloud toward the end of their days.

During Asa’s single reign in Judah, the government of Israel was in six or seven different hands. Observe the ruin of Jeroboam’s family; no word of God will fail. Divine threats are not intended merely to terrify. Ungodly people carry out the just judgments of God upon each other. But in the midst of terrible sins and this apparent confusion, the Lord carries on his own plan: when it is fully completed, the glorious justice, wisdom, truth, and mercy displayed therein will be admired and adored through all the ages of eternity.
